ops.title: Go XPC/Mach audit-trailer transport for connection-bound peer identity (T1 follow-up)
ops.description: Deferred per user decision 2026-07-18 (see note 97c5f2a5 + the T1 decision note). daemonkit's Go unix-socket peer trust (trust.Policy + LOCAL_PEERTOKEN) is best-effort defense-in-depth atop the same-UID floor: it has a residual same-UID TOCTOU because LOCAL_PEERTOKEN is query-time (verified vs XNU uipc_ctloutput), not connection-bound. The sound primitive is a kernel per-message audit trailer (XPC/Mach) — the Swift side already uses NSXPCConnection.setCodeSigningRequirement. This task: prototype a cgo-free (purego/Mach) Go XPC or Mach-message transport that delivers a connection/message-bound audit token, report feasibility + cost, then a go/no-go on adopting it for trust-bearing lifecycle ops. Not on the F→I→P→K→S critical path; pick it up when a surface needs connection-bound identity, or when scheduling allows. Trust ships now with the honest limitation documented in trust/ + the accept decision recorded.
